reinforcementResults.summary: Based on the current workspace code and test artifacts, this feature satisfies the active policy: MCP uses OAuth service-account authentication with tool-level RBAC (plus strictly validated short-lived bridge keys), MCP write authorization is audited, MCP execution reuses shared HTTP/domain handlers, and payroll/order/audit retention-deletion workflows are implemented with schedulable jobs, encryption and access-control test coverage, and audit-query traceability of purge outcomes.

excludedApproaches: Network-trust-only authentication for MCP access.
A separate MCP-only domain logic implementation.
Unscoped broad MCP credentials without tool-level RBAC.
executionConstraints: Use OAuth service-account + tool-level RBAC as the core MCP authz model.
Support optional short-lived key bridging only under strict rotation and audit controls.
MCP must call the same domain services used by HTTP APIs.
features.name: MCP Server Domain Parity
features.rationale: Agent integration is a required first-class surface with strict security parity needs.
features.summary: Implement secure MCP access over shared domain logic and permission controls.

acceptanceSignals: Feature is disabled by default in MVP environments.
Enabling recommendations does not remove deterministic search/filter behavior.
Recommendation failures do not block ordering flows.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Recommendation Engine in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The recommendation feature is correctly behind a runtime flag (`PRELAUNCH_RECOMMENDATION_ENGINE_ENABLED`) that defaults to OFF, is implemented as an additive ranking layer on top of existing deterministic filtering/sorting, and degrades gracefully when ranking fails without blocking ordering. The externally visible API changes are synchronized across canonical contract (`src/contract.rs`), published OpenAPI artifacts, generated TypeScript client models, and contract-conformance CI checks (including route-to-spec coverage and contract verification).

reinforcementResults.name: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: Recommendation capability is implemented behind a runtime feature flag that defaults OFF (`PRELAUNCH_RECOMMENDATION_ENGINE_ENABLED` parses to `false` when unset), applied only as an additive ranking layer after deterministic filtering/sorting, and guarded so recommendation errors degrade gracefully without blocking ordering. This feature adds no new high-risk write path, while the existing immutable audit model in the workspace already enforces actorId/role/entityId/reason/timestamp/correlationId capture with append-only evidence sequencing.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: The recommendation engine feature satisfies the active policy: it is gated by a runtime flag defaulting to OFF, recommendation is applied as an additive ranking step after deterministic filtering/sorting, recommendation failures degrade to deterministic ordering without blocking order creation, and superseded compatibility paths (recommendation query DTO/parameter) were removed in the same feature batch.

reinforcementResults.name: Optional Feature Flag Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: The current recommendation feature satisfies the active policy: it is gated by a runtime flag (`PRELAUNCH_RECOMMENDATION_ENGINE_ENABLED`) parsed with default `false`, recommendation is applied only as an additive ranking step after deterministic filtering/sorting, and recommendation failures explicitly degrade to deterministic ordering without blocking ordering flows (covered by dedicated tests for flag-off behavior, deterministic ranking, and failure isolation).

reinforcementResults.name: Data Retention Enforcement
reinforcementResults.summary: The workspace satisfies the Recommendation Engine feature requirements and the active retention/deletion policy: recommendation is runtime-gated default OFF, applied only as additive ranking with deterministic fallback, failures do not block ordering, and payroll/order/audit retention workflows (scheduled purge paths, encryption/access-control coverage, and audit-query traceability of purge outcomes) are implemented with test evidence.

excludedApproaches: Always-on recommendation in MVP rollout.
Replacing core filtering with recommendation-only ranking.
Introducing separate source-of-truth state for recommendation outputs.
executionConstraints: Ship behind runtime feature flag default OFF.
Treat recommendation as additive ranking layer only.
Preserve deterministic discovery behavior when flag is OFF.
features.name: Recommendation Engine
features.rationale: Recommendations are phase-2 value add and must not destabilize MVP.
features.summary: Provide optional recommendation capability behind controlled rollout.

acceptanceSignals: Reminder scheduling supports preorder-open and demand-spike scenarios.
Users can opt out and preferences are enforced.
Reminder delivery failures do not impact ordering correctness.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Rush Reminder Notifications in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The new HTTP behavior (`PUT /api/v1/employee/rush-reminder-preferences`) is synchronized with OpenAPI (`contract/openapi/openapi.yaml` and `openapi.json`) and generated client artifacts (`contract/generated/ts-client/*`) in the same change set, and contract tests include route-to-spec coverage plus explicit error-response schema assertions for the new operation.

reinforcementResults.name: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: Rush Reminder Notifications are correctly gated behind a runtime feature flag defaulting to OFF, scheduling is policy-driven (preorder-open and demand-spike) with throttling and preference opt-out enforcement, and reminder delivery failures are handled best-effort without impacting ordering correctness. The feature does not introduce a new high-risk write path that bypasses the existing immutable audit-trail model.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: Pass. The workspace implements Rush Reminder Notifications behind `PRELAUNCH_RUSH_REMINDER_ENABLED` (default OFF), adds policy-driven scheduling for preorder-open and demand-spike scenarios with throttling and opt-out preference enforcement, and isolates reminder scheduling/delivery as best-effort so delivery failures do not affect ordering correctness. The feature diff shows new contracts/routes/models without retaining superseded legacy/fallback reminder paths.

reinforcementResults.name: Optional Feature Flag Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: The current feature satisfies the active policy: rush reminders are controlled by a runtime flag (`PRELAUNCH_RUSH_REMINDER_ENABLED`) that defaults to OFF, reminder API exposure is disabled when the flag is OFF (route not mounted and handler returns 404), scheduling is policy-driven with preorder-open and demand-spike scenarios plus throttling and preference opt-out enforcement, and delivery is best-effort/isolated so failures do not affect ordering correctness.

reinforcementResults.name: Data Retention Enforcement
reinforcementResults.summary: Rush Reminder Notifications in this workspace is feature-flagged at runtime with default OFF, supports preorder-open and demand-spike scheduling with policy throttling and opt-out preference enforcement, and keeps reminder delivery best-effort/isolated from ordering paths. The active payroll/order/audit data policy is also technically enforced via retention+purge workflows (including scheduled purge jobs), sensitive payroll field encryption with access-control coverage, and audit-query traceability for purge outcomes.

excludedApproaches: Making reminder delivery a prerequisite for successful ordering.
Sending unthrottled notifications without preference controls.
Hardcoding one static reminder strategy for all contexts.
executionConstraints: Ship behind runtime feature flag default OFF.
Use policy-driven scheduling with throttling and preference checks.
Keep reminder workflows operationally isolated from transaction paths.
features.name: Rush Reminder Notifications
features.rationale: Reminder capability is phase-2 and should remain non-critical to MVP flow.
features.summary: Add optional notification support for open-window and rush cases.

acceptanceSignals: Dashboards provide vendor/plant/time breakdown with metric definitions.
Feature-off state removes dashboard UI/API exposure.
Analytics queries run on governed aggregates without harming transactional workloads.

attempts.latestEventSummary: feature completed: Advanced Analytics Dashboard in 0 attempts

reinforcementResults.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcementResults.summary: The active policy is satisfied for this feature: the new analytics HTTP endpoints are synchronized across runtime route contracts and OpenAPI (`openapi.yaml`/`openapi.json`), generated TypeScript client artifacts are updated in the same change set, and contract tests validate route-to-spec coverage plus error response schema mappings for the new endpoints. The feature is runtime-flagged default OFF and implemented on pre-aggregated, versioned analytics models.

reinforcementResults.name: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: The Advanced Analytics Dashboard implementation satisfies the active policy for this feature: it is runtime-flagged default OFF, dashboard endpoints are unavailable when disabled, analytics reads use a pre-aggregated warehouse with versioned metric definitions, and high-risk operational writes remain covered by immutable audit evidence containing actorId/role/entityId/reason/timestamp with correlationId linkage.

reinforcementResults.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcementResults.summary: The Advanced Analytics Dashboard implementation satisfies the active policy for this feature: it is runtime-gated by `PRELAUNCH_ADVANCED_ANALYTICS_DASHBOARD_ENABLED` with default OFF, dashboard routes are not mounted when disabled (and handler-level access returns 404), responses include vendor/plant/time breakdowns plus versioned metric definitions (`key/displayName/unit/formula/source/version`), and query execution reads pre-aggregated warehouse state rather than transactional tables while governance workflows remain the authoritative action path.

reinforcementResults.name: Optional Feature Flag Discipline
reinforcementResults.summary: The current Advanced Analytics Dashboard implementation satisfies the active optional-phase policy: it is gated by runtime flag `PRELAUNCH_ADVANCED_ANALYTICS_DASHBOARD_ENABLED` with default OFF, dashboard routes are not registered when disabled (and handler-level access also returns `404`), metric outputs are versioned and include formula/source definitions, and query reads come from a pre-aggregated `OperationsAnalyticsWarehouse` model rather than direct transactional-table analytics paths.

reinforcementResults.name: Data Retention Enforcement
reinforcementResults.summary: The current feature satisfies the active policy: advanced analytics is behind a runtime flag default OFF with endpoint gating, uses versioned metric definitions over pre-aggregated analytical state, and does not bypass existing enforced payroll/order/audit retention-purge workflows, encryption/access-control coverage, or audit-query traceability for deletion outcomes.

excludedApproaches: Replacing governance workflow actions with dashboard-only monitoring.
Querying hot transactional tables directly for interactive analytics.
Publishing KPI views without formula/source documentation.
executionConstraints: Ship behind runtime feature flag default OFF.
Use versioned metric definitions and pre-aggregated analytical models.
Keep operational governance workflow as primary action path.
features.name: Advanced Analytics Dashboard
features.rationale: Deep analytics is phase-2 and should build on stable MVP data contracts.
features.summary: Provide optional advanced operations analytics for admin and vendor views.

activation.rationale: 正式 HTTP 契約與契約測試基礎完成後，所有後續 API 變更都必須受同一門檻約束。

checks: CI route-to-spec diff 無未登錄正式 endpoint。
OpenAPI 變更與生成型別/SDK 同 PR 提交。
錯誤碼與錯誤 schema 對應可被測試驗證。

reinforcements.instructions: 新增或修改正式 HTTP 行為時，必須同步更新契約與生成產物，否則拒絕合併。
reinforcements.name: HTTP Contract Sync Gate
reinforcements.policy: No externally used HTTP behavior may ship unless OpenAPI and generated artifacts are synchronized in the same change set.

activation.rationale: MCP 功能啟用時最容易發生規則分叉，需立即強制同源邏輯檢查。

checks: 每個 MCP write tool 可映射到共享 domain service。
MCP/HTTP 配對測試顯示驗證與錯誤行為一致。
不存在 MCP 專屬權限繞過路徑。

reinforcements.instructions: PR review 必須附 MCP tool-to-service 對照與權限驗證證據。
reinforcements.name: MCP Domain Parity Gate
reinforcements.policy: MCP tools/resources must reuse HTTP domain services and the same authorization/validation rules.

activation.rationale: 審計查詢能力完成後，所有高風險寫入都應進入統一不可竄改留痕。

checks: 高風險寫入事件包含 actorId、role、entityId、reason、timestamp。
審計紀錄採 append-only 或等效不可竄改模式。
可用 correlationId 串連跨流程操作鏈。

reinforcements.instructions: 下單、核銷、審核、退款、鎖帳等動作若缺少必要審計欄位不得上線。
reinforcements.name: High-Risk Write Audit Discipline
reinforcements.policy: All high-risk write operations must emit immutable, attributable audit events.

activation.rationale: 專案明確不追求向後相容，任何替代路徑都會增加維運風險。

checks: 變更後不存在 legacy/fallback 分支維持舊正式行為。
被取代的 handler/DTO/route 在同批次移除。
測試僅覆蓋現行正式流程。

reinforcements.instructions: 每次破壞式調整必須同步刪除舊路徑與舊測試。
reinforcements.name: No Legacy Path Rule
reinforcements.policy: Superseded compatibility and fallback paths must be removed immediately when new contracts ship.

activation.rationale: 第二階段能力必須與 MVP 核心路徑隔離，避免影響首階段驗收。

checks: Optional 功能在 MVP 環境預設 flag=false。
Flag 關閉時無對外入口與副作用 API。
具備可驗證的一鍵停用路徑。

reinforcements.instructions: Optional 功能 PR 需附 flag 設定與關閉驗證結果。
reinforcements.name: Optional Feature Flag Discipline
reinforcements.policy: All optional phase-2 capabilities must ship behind runtime feature flags defaulted OFF for MVP.

activation.rationale: 交易與核銷是尖峰風險核心，該能力屬於訂單與核銷功能本體。

checks: 壓測報告覆蓋尖峰場景且達成既定 p95/p99 與錯誤率門檻。
尖峰測試含下單與核銷並發場景。
Grafana 面板可即時觀測 SLO 指標。

reinforcements.instructions: 未通過硬性 SLO gate 不得進入 production。
reinforcements.name: Peak Load SLO Gate
reinforcements.policy: Order and pickup capabilities must pass hard pre-launch peak-load SLO validation.

activation.rationale: 財務能力啟用時需立即防止錯帳與不可追溯調整。

checks: 每期結算快照有唯一 cycle key 與摘要校驗。
同 cycle 重送具可驗證 idempotency。
例外清單完整覆蓋退款、扣款失敗、離職員工。

reinforcements.instructions: 結算上線前完成鎖帳、重跑、例外演練並保留證據。
reinforcements.name: Financial Close Integrity Gate
reinforcements.policy: Settlement and payroll outputs must be deterministic, idempotent, and fully auditable.

activation.rationale: 資料政策已決議，完成審計與薪資資料落地後應全面啟用。

checks: 保留年限、刪除/匿名化工作排程可被驗證。
高敏欄位加密與存取控制有測試覆蓋。
刪除/匿名化結果可於稽核查詢中追蹤。

reinforcements.instructions: 將資料保留與刪除政策落地為可執行工作流與測試，不接受文件化未落地。
reinforcements.name: Data Retention Enforcement
reinforcements.policy: Defined retention and deletion policy must be technically enforced for payroll, order, and audit data.
